Chip Cooper, Esq. (614-481-6000; chipc@cooperelliott.com) of Cooper & Elliott, LLC for Plaintiff, Zaif Inc.
Grant Fondo, Esq. (650-752-3100; gfondo@goodwinlaw.com) for Defendant, BAM Trading Services, Inc.
Roberto Gonzalez, Esq. (202-223-7316; rgonzalez@paulweiss.com) for Defendant, Binance Holdings Ltd.
Chris Stecher, Esq. (415-398-6000; christopher.stecher@kyl.com) of Keesal, Yound & Logan for Defendant, Binance Holdings Ltd.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Specially appearing defendant Binance Holdings Ltd.'s motion to dismiss for inconvenient forum, or in the alternative, motion to quash for lack of personal jurisdiction is continued to allow for jurisdictional discovery. (See Mihlon v. Superior Court (1985) 169 Cal.App.3d 703 [plaintiff entitled to conduct discovery with regard to the issue of jurisdiction before the hearing on the motion to quash].) "In order to prevail on a motion for a continuance for jurisdictional discovery, the plaintiff should demonstrate that discovery is likely to lead to the production of evidence of facts establishing jurisdiction." (In re Automobile Antitrust Cases I & II (2005) 135 Cal.App.4th 100, 127.) Here, Zaif shows that further discovery is likely to lead to the production of evidence establishing personal jurisdiction against Binance Holdings. Zaif additionally shows that discovery will produce evidence needed for the forum non conveniens analysis. The specific and targeted jurisdictional discovery requested by Zaif, such as the location of servers and storage devices, may establish jurisdiction and be relevant to the alter ego and forum non conveniens motion.

The motion is continued to Friday, October 15, 2021. Supplemental memo due September 22, not exceeding 10 pages. Supplemental opposition due October 1, not exceeding ten (10) pages. Supplemental reply due October 7, not exceeding five (5) pages.

Order is signed

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
